title: Winter Paralympics: 6 Fun Sports for Athletes
summary: The Winter Paralympics have started in Italy with a big opening ceremony. Over 600 athletes are competing for 79 medals in six exciting sports. These sports are Para alpine skiing, Para biathlon, Para cross-country skiing, Para ice hockey, Para snowboard, and wheelchair curling. In skiing sports, athletes can compete in three groups: standing, sitting on a special ski, or with vision impairment using a guide. They race in different events like slalom and downhill. The games began in 1976 and are held in cool places like Cortina. It's all about showing amazing skills and having fun in the snow!

Games or activities like skiing and hockey.
Prizes like gold, silver, or bronze for winning.
A country in Europe where the games are happening.
Sliding on snow using skis for fun or competition.

background_read: The Winter Paralympics are a special sports event for athletes with disabilities, held every four years in winter. It started in 1976 to give everyone a chance to compete in sports like skiing and hockey. The games promote inclusion and show how athletes overcome challenges. They are similar to the Olympics but focus on adapting sports for different abilities. Learning about this helps kids understand diversity and teamwork in sports.
Article_Structure: WHO: Over 600 Para athletes with disabilities
WHAT: Competing in six winter sports for medals
WHERE: In Italy, with events in places like Cortina
WHY: To showcase skills, promote inclusion, and have fun in sports

perspectives.description: Athletes train hard to compete and show their abilities in adapted sports.
